revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]][[ja:和泉黄金塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Izumi Koganezuka Kofun | native_name =和泉黄金塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Izumikoganeduka Kofun 20080720.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Izumi Koganezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Izumi Koganezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.514166666667|N|135.45583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumi|qid=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=pink|text align=center|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}}} {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|25|42.95|N|135|21|19|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Izumi Koganezuka Kofun'''|和泉黄金塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Uedai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|qid=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka|1=Izumi, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003696|title= 和泉黄金塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is noted for the [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s found within. These and other artifacts excavated from the tumulus are collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/531621|title=黄金塚古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs<ref name="Izumi Northern Lineage">"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉の Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70.</ref><!--took from [[Ozono_Kofun]]--> !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|qid=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60–70m |Early 5th century |- |South |Ōtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''Ōzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |} The Izumi Koganezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|[[Zenpokoenfun]]|前方後円墳|3=|4=|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the northwestern end of Shintayama hill, surrounded [[pa11y field]]s, but the location is also close to the ancient [[kokufu|provincial capital]] of former {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}. The tumulus has a total length of about 94 meters. The presence of a ''kofun'' at this location has been known since antiquity, but in November 1945, a 17-year-old student noticed that the tumulus was severely damaged and convinced local authorities that a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|qid=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} excavation be performed. At that time, a leather-lacquered shield with copper decorations, iron swords and other metal artifacts were discovered. In 1950 to 1951, further excavation of the posterior circular portion by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and the Japanese Archaeological Association found the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] within which three clay-covered wooden caskets were found side by side. The central casket had the remains of a woman, and she was flanked on either side with the remains of two men. Within and surrounding these coffins were a large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' mirrors, ''[[magatama]]'' beads and other jewelry, fragments of armor and a helm, iron swords, spears and other weapons, and other artifacts. The inscription of one of the mirror gave the reign date of Keisho 3, which corresponds to 238 AD. This drew great attention by historians, as per the Chinese chronicle {{transliteration|zh|Wei zhi}} ({{lang|zh|魏志}} "Records of Wei"), which is part of the ''{{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|qid=Q247318|lt=Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Records of the Three Kingdoms}}'' ({{lang|zh|三國志}}), in the year 239 AD Emperor {{ill|Cao Rui|en|Cao Rui|qid=Q378470|lt=Cao Rui|1=Cao Rui}} sent "one hundred bronze mirrors" to Queen [[Himiko]] of [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]],<ref>Goodrich, Carrington C, ed. (1951). Japan in the Chinese Dynastic Histories: Later Han Through Ming Dynasties. Translated by Tsunoda, Ryusaku. South Pasadena: PD and Ione Perkins. p. 15</ref> and it was possible that this was one of these mirrors. These excavated items are now are in the possession of the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Further excavation were conducted from 2001 to 2005 by the Izumi City Board of Education which confirmed the scale of the tumulus, and recovered many cylindrical and figurative ''haniwa''. From the ''haniwa'' and other artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was completed in the latter half of the 4th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tonoki Station|en|Tonoki Station|qid=Q857450|lt=Tonoki Station|1=Tonoki Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 94 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 42 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 57 meter diameter x 9 meters high, 2-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
